# BigHand Data Product

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | **Gold inventory:** [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/bighand-gold-layer-exploration]] | Ingestion: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/api-ingestion-notebooks]] | Person facts: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/worker-data-mart]] | Naming: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/dlz-naming-and-schema-conventions]] | **Holidays:** [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]]

## Summary

**BigHand** is a DLZ **ingestion source** (`bighand`) and a small **data product** that retains one entity only: **Offices** (Enriched Dataset → `silver.temp`).

Three person-level facts and one office-level holiday fact sourced from BigHand BASE tables are **WorkerDataMart** entities (`FactPersonForecast`, `FactPersonQualification`, `FactPersonWorkingArrangement`, `FactPublicHoliday`) with CURATED tables in `gold.workerdatamart` and MODEL views including `FactPersonQualification`, `FactPersonCapacity`, and `FactPublicHoliday`.

## Current entity catalogue

| Entity | Product | Type | Terminal output |
|--------|---------|------|-----------------|
| `Offices` | **BigHand** | Enriched Dataset | `silver.temp.enriched_bighand_offices` |
| `FactPersonForecast` | **WorkerDataMart** | Fact | `workerdatamart.curated_factpersonforecast` |
| `FactPersonQualification` | **WorkerDataMart** | Fact | `workerdatamart.curated_factpersonqualification` + MODEL `FactPersonQualification` |
| `FactPersonWorkingArrangement` | **WorkerDataMart** | Fact | `workerdatamart.curated_factpersonworkingarrangement` |
| `FactPublicHoliday` | **WorkerDataMart** | Fact | `workerdatamart.curated_factpublicholiday` + MODEL `FactPublicHoliday` |
| — | **WorkerDataMart** | MODEL view | `workerdatamart.factpersoncapacity` (composite person×date) |

Ingestion BASE (silver): `bighand.bighand_forecasts`, `bighand_offices`, `bighand_qualifications`, `bighand_workingarrangements`, `bighand_publicholidays` (5 tables).

## Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph ingest [Ingestion]
    ADF["ADF: Task - Ingest BigHand"]
    NB["Ingest BigHand.py"]
    RAW["ADLS RAW"]
  end
  subgraph silver [silver]
    BASE["bighand.bighand_*"]
    OFF_TEMP["temp.enriched_bighand_offices"]
  end
  subgraph gold [gold]
    WDM_ENR["temp.enriched_workerdatamart_factperson*|factpublicholiday"]
    WDM_CUR["workerdatamart.curated_factperson*|factpublicholiday"]
    VIEWS["factpersonqualification / factpersoncapacity / factpublicholiday"]
  end

  ADF --> NB --> RAW --> BASE
  BASE --> OFF_TEMP
  BASE --> WDM_ENR --> WDM_CUR --> VIEWS
```

| Stage | BigHand product (Offices) | WDM facts |
|-------|-------------------------|-----------|
| **Ingestion** | API → RAW via ADF + `Ingest BigHand.py` | Same BASE tables; `PublicHolidays` dual-year GETs |
| **RAW→BASE** | `RAW to BASE - Default` | Same |
| **ENRICHED** | Cast/prune → `silver.temp` | PersonKey / office fan-out / DateKey → `gold.temp` |
| **CURATED** | — (terminal at ENRICHED) | Mechanical load → `workerdatamart.curated_*` |
| **MODEL** | — | Hand-authored views in `workerdatamart` |

### PersonKey join (WDM ENRICHED person facts)

```sql
INNER JOIN `{GoldCatalog}`.workerdatamart.curated_dimperson dp
  ON bh.<employeeId column> = dp.EmployeeId
 AND dp.is_current = 1
 AND lower(dp.Source) = 'workday'
INNER JOIN `{GoldCatalog}`.workerdatamart.curated_dimoffice o
  ON lower(o.OfficeId) = lower(dp.PrimaryOfficeId)
 AND o.is_current = 1
 AND lower(o.Source) = 'workday'
 AND o.OfficeRegionCode NOT IN ('CE', 'UK and Ireland')
```

Use `lower()` on office IDs. Join `curated_dimperson`, not MODEL `DimPerson` views. **UK/EU filter does not apply to `FactPublicHoliday`** (office holidays).

### FactPublicHoliday

- **Grain:** `DateKey` + `OfficeKey` + `HolidayName`
- Dual-year ingest: `GET PublicHolidays/{currentYear}/` + `GET PublicHolidays/{nextYear}/`, dedupe
- **Operational guide:** [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]] — validation, office coverage gaps, consumer joins, API year coverage

## Contracts

| Scope | Path |
|-------|------|
| Source system | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/bighand/` |
| BigHand product | `metadata/contracts/data-products/BigHand/` (`Offices` only) |
| WDM facts | `metadata/contracts/data-products/WorkerDataMart/FactPerson*.json`, `FactPublicHoliday.json` |
| CSV sources | `metadata/contracts/scripts/ingestion/bighand-*.csv`, `.../data-products/BigHand-*.csv`, WDM entity/column CSV rows |

Regenerate ingestion JSON: `metadata/contracts/scripts/ingestion/Generate-DataContractsFromSchemaCSV.py` (local dev) or PowerShell `Generate-DataContractsFromSchemaCSV.ps1` (CI).

## Repo artifacts

### Ingestion (ADF)

| Artifact | Role |
|----------|------|
| `ingestion/.../pipeline/Task - Ingest BigHand.json` | ADF task → Databricks notebook |
| `ingestion/.../linkedService/BigHand_Source.json` | REST linked service (KV secrets) |
| `ingestion/.../dataset/BigHand_Source.json` | Dataset for BigHand API |
| `Child - Ingest Data.json` | Routes `BigHand API` → `Task - Ingest BigHand` |

### Databricks notebooks

| Stage | Path |
|-------|------|
| API ingest | `11 - Ingestion/Ingest BigHand.py` |
| ENRICHED Offices | `3 - ENRICHED/BigHand/Enriched - BigHand - Enriched Dataset - Offices.py` |
| ENRICHED facts | `3 - ENRICHED/Worker Data Mart/Enriched - Worker Data Mart - Fact - Person {Forecast\|Qualification\|Working Arrangement}.py`, `... Public Holiday.py` |
| CURATED facts | `4 - CURATED/Worker Data Mart/Curated - Worker Data Mart - Fact - Person *.py`, `... Public Holiday.py` |
| MODEL | `5 - MODEL/Worker Data Mart/Model - Worker Data Mart - Fact - Person {Qualification\|Capacity}.py`, `... Public Holiday.py` |
| BASE DDL | `1 - Post Deployment/Create BASE Metastore Tables - bighand.py` |
| CURATED DDL | `1 - Post Deployment/Create CURATED Metastore Tables - WorkerDataMart - BigHand Facts.py` |

Post-deploy orchestrators `%run` the BASE and CURATED DDL children from `Create BASE Metastore Tables.py` and `Create CURATED Metastore Tables.py`. MODEL notebooks register in `Run All Model Views.py`.

## Deploy sequence

| Order | Pipeline | Notes |
|------:|----------|-------|
| 1 | **Metadata** | Contracts → SQL orchestration; can run parallel with Transformation |
| 2 | **Transformation** | `import-dir` notebooks to `/Data Platform` |
| 3 | **Unity Catalog** | Post-deploy DDL (~45 min); **never** parallel with Transformation |
| 4 | **Main Orchestrator** | Load ENRICHED → CURATED |
| 5 | **Run All Model Views** | If not included in UC run |

Prerequisites: `DimPerson` / `DimOffice` / `DimDate` curated; BASE populated for all five `bighand_*` entities.

See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/deployment-and-ci-cd]] for pipeline detail and [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]] for ADF chain.

## Validation SQL (dev reference)

See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday#Validation SQL]] for full holiday-fact checks. Quick row counts:

arguments.content:
# FactPersonCalendar

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/worker-data-mart]] | [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] | [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]]

## Summary

**`workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar`** is a **gold-layer SQL view** (not a contracted Delta entity) that builds a **person × date calendar** with employment, leave, holiday, and office-attendance flags. It lives in Unity Catalog catalog `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold`, schema `workerdatamart`.

> **Not in repo:** No contract, notebook, or deployment script exists in `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`. The view was created directly in the workspace (owner: martin.sikula@freshfields.com, created 2026-06-22).

## Unity Catalog name

| Layer | Full name |
|-------|-----------|
| Gold view | `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold.workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` |
| Shorthand (used in notebooks) | `workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` |

**Type:** VIEW (not a managed Delta table). No `curated_` prefix — unlike most WDM contracted facts.

## Curation stages

This object **does not** follow the standard RAW → BASE → ENRICHED → CURATED → MODEL pipeline as a contracted entity. Instead it is a **downstream analytical view** that reads already-curated upstream tables:

```
RAW → BASE (silver UC)     ─┐
ENRICHED (CDF Lookup)      ─┼→ gold VIEW: workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar
CURATED (WDM dimensions)   ─┘
```

| Stage | Role for this view |
|-------|--------------------|
| **RAW → BASE** | Source ingestion tables land in silver catalog |
| **ENRICHED** | `OfficeCrosswalk` (CDF Lookup) used for holiday office mapping |
| **CURATED** | WDM `DimDate`, `DimPerson`, `DimOffice` provide conformed keys |
| **MODEL** | N/A — view is exposed directly in gold `workerdatamart` schema |

## Source tables (upstream)

### Silver / BASE (`fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-silver`)

| Schema | Table | Ingestion contract | Purpose |
|--------|-------|-------------------|----------|
| `workday` | `workday_availability` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_availability.json` | Scheduled hours, availability per colleague per date |
| `workday` | `workday_leave_of_absence` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_leave_of_absence.json` | Secondment and other leave |
| `workday` | `workday_colleague` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_colleague.json` | Email / Workday username → Colleague_ID mapping |
| `elite3e` | `elite3e_officeholiday` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/elite3e/elite3e_officeholiday.json` | Office public holidays |
| `elite3e` | `elite3e_office` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/elite3e/elite3e_office.json` | Office site codes for holidays |
| `nexthink` | `nexthink_session_events` | **Not in repo** | Endpoint/session events for office vs online attendance |
| `temp` | `enriched_cdflookup_officecrosswalk` | ENRICHED `CDFLookup.OfficeCrosswalk` | Elite3E site → Workday office name crosswalk |

### Gold / CURATED WDM (`fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold.workerdatamart`)

| Table | Contract entity | Notebook area |
|-------|----------------|---------------|
| `curated_dimdate` | DimDate (post-deployment) | `4 - CURATED/Worker Data Mart/Post Deployment/` |
| `curated_dimperson` | DimPerson | `3 - ENRICHED` → `4 - CURATED` |
| `curated_dimoffice` | DimOffice | `3 - ENRICHED` → `4 - CURATED` |

## View logic (high level)

The view SQL composes four CTEs:

1. **`colleagues`** — deduplicated Workday colleague lookup by email / username
2. **`attendance`** — Nexthink session events joined to DimDate, DimPerson, DimOffice
3. **`holidays`** — Elite3E office holidays crosswalked to Workday office names
4. **`calendar`** — person × date spine from `workday_availability` with holiday, employment, leave, and vacation flags
5. **`office_attendance`** — merges calendar with attendance to set `IsPrimaryOfficeAttendance`, `IsOtherOfficeAttendance`, `IsOnline`

## Output columns

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `DateKey` | Surrogate date key (from `curated_dimdate`) |
| `PersonKey` | Surrogate person key (Workday source) |
| `PrimaryOfficeKey` | Person's primary office on that date |
| `SecondaryOfficeKey` | Person's secondary office on that date |
| `IsHolidayInPrimaryOffice` | Public holiday in primary office |
| `IsHolidayInSecondaryOffice` | Public holiday in secondary office |
| `IsEmployeeNonWorkingDay` | Weekday with zero scheduled hours |
| `IsEmployed` | Date on or after person's effective start |
| `IsOnSecondment` | On secondment leave |
| `IsOnOtherLeave` | On non-secondment leave |
| `IsOnVacation` | Scheduled hours exceed available hours after absence |
| `IsPrimaryOfficeAttendance` | Physical attendance at primary office (Nexthink) |
| `IsOtherOfficeAttendance` | Physical attendance at a non-primary office |
| `IsOnline` | Online session with no mapped office |

## Related contracted entities

| Entity | Relationship |
|--------|-------------|
| `FactWorkerAvailability` | Shares `workday_availability` source; contracted fact with truncate-and-reload |
| `FactTimesheet` | Also uses `elite3e_officeholiday` and calendar logic for missing timesheets |
| `FactPublicHoliday` | BigHand-sourced WDM fact (`curated_factpublicholiday`) — office×date holiday grain; **operational guide:** [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]]; refactor of this calendar view to consume it is **deferred** |
| `DimPerson`, `DimOffice`, `DimDate` | Direct dependencies |

## Agent notes

- Query with `DESCRIBE TABLE EXTENDED workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` to inspect view SQL if definition drifts.
- Nexthink ingestion is **workspace-only** — not tracked in this repo.
- Do not assume `factpersoncalendar` has a `curated_` or `model_` counterpart; it is a standalone view.
- For **office-level** BigHand holidays (not person flags), use [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]] instead.

ENRICHED (CDF Lookup)      ─┼→ gold VIEW: workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar
CURATED (WDM dimensions)   ─┘
```

| Stage | Role for this view |
|-------|--------------------|
| **RAW → BASE** | Source ingestion tables land in silver catalog |
| **ENRICHED** | `OfficeCrosswalk` (CDF Lookup) used for holiday office mapping |
| **CURATED** | WDM `DimDate`, `DimPerson`, `DimOffice` provide conformed keys |
| **MODEL** | N/A — view is exposed directly in gold `workerdatamart` schema |

## Source tables (upstream)

### Silver / BASE (`fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-silver`)

| Schema | Table | Ingestion contract | Purpose |
|--------|-------|-------------------|----------|
| `workday` | `workday_availability` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_availability.json` | Scheduled hours, availability per colleague per date |
| `workday` | `workday_leave_of_absence` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_leave_of_absence.json` | Secondment and other leave |
| `workday` | `workday_colleague` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/workday/workday_colleague.json` | Email / Workday username → Colleague_ID mapping |
| `elite3e` | `elite3e_officeholiday` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/elite3e/elite3e_officeholiday.json` | Office public holidays |
| `elite3e` | `elite3e_office` | `metadata/contracts/ingestion/elite3e/elite3e_office.json` | Office site codes for holidays |
| `nexthink` | `nexthink_session_events` | **Not in repo** | Endpoint/session events for office vs online attendance |
| `temp` | `enriched_cdflookup_officecrosswalk` | ENRICHED `CDFLookup.OfficeCrosswalk` | Elite3E site → Workday office name crosswalk |

### Gold / CURATED WDM (`fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold.workerdatamart`)

| Table | Contract entity | Notebook area |
|-------|----------------|---------------|
| `curated_dimdate` | DimDate (post-deployment) | `4 - CURATED/Worker Data Mart/Post Deployment/` |
| `curated_dimperson` | DimPerson | `3 - ENRICHED` → `4 - CURATED` |
| `curated_dimoffice` | DimOffice | `3 - ENRICHED` → `4 - CURATED` |

## View logic (high level)

The view SQL composes four CTEs:

1. **`colleagues`** — deduplicated Workday colleague lookup by email / username
2. **`attendance`** — Nexthink session events joined to DimDate, DimPerson, DimOffice
3. **`holidays`** — Elite3E office holidays crosswalked to Workday office names
4. **`calendar`** — person × date spine from `workday_availability` with holiday, employment, leave, and vacation flags
5. **`office_attendance`** — merges calendar with attendance to set `IsPrimaryOfficeAttendance`, `IsOtherOfficeAttendance`, `IsOnline`

## Output columns

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `DateKey` | Surrogate date key (from `curated_dimdate`) |
| `PersonKey` | Surrogate person key (Workday source) |
| `PrimaryOfficeKey` | Person's primary office on that date |
| `SecondaryOfficeKey` | Person's secondary office on that date |
| `IsHolidayInPrimaryOffice` | Public holiday in primary office |
| `IsHolidayInSecondaryOffice` | Public holiday in secondary office |
| `IsEmployeeNonWorkingDay` | Weekday with zero scheduled hours |
| `IsEmployed` | Date on or after person's effective start |
| `IsOnSecondment` | On secondment leave |
| `IsOnOtherLeave` | On non-secondment leave |
| `IsOnVacation` | Scheduled hours exceed available hours after absence |
| `IsPrimaryOfficeAttendance` | Physical attendance at primary office (Nexthink) |
| `IsOtherOfficeAttendance` | Physical attendance at a non-primary office |
| `IsOnline` | Online session with no mapped office |

## Related contracted entities

| Entity | Relationship |
|--------|-------------|
| `FactWorkerAvailability` | Shares `workday_availability` source; contracted fact with truncate-and-reload |
| `FactTimesheet` | Also uses `elite3e_officeholiday` and calendar logic for missing timesheets |
| `FactPublicHoliday` | BigHand-sourced WDM fact (`curated_factpublicholiday`) — office×date holiday grain; **operational guide:** [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]]; refactor of this calendar view to consume it is **deferred** |
| `DimPerson`, `DimOffice`, `DimDate` | Direct dependencies |

## Agent notes

- Query with `DESCRIBE TABLE EXTENDED workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` to inspect view SQL if definition drifts.
- Nexthink ingestion is **workspace-only** — not tracked in this repo.
- Do not assume `factpersoncalendar` has a `curated_` or `model_` counterpart; it is a standalone view.
- For **office-level** BigHand holidays (not person flags), use [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/factpublicholiday]] instead.

## Summary

**`workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar`** is a **gold-layer SQL view** (not a contracted Delta entity) that builds a **person × date calendar** with employment, leave, holiday, and office-attendance flags. It lives in Unity Catalog catalog `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold`, schema `workerdatamart`.

> **Not in repo:** No contract, notebook, or deployment script exists in `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`. The view was created directly in the workspace (owner: martin.sikula@freshfields.com, created 2026-06-22).

## Unity Catalog name

| Gold view | `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw-gold.workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` |
| Shorthand (used in notebooks) | `workerdatamart.factpersoncalendar` |

**Type:** VIEW (not a managed Delta table). No `curated_` prefix — unlike most WDM contracted facts.

arguments.content:
# Data Platform DLZ

> **Audience**: Cursor agents (and humans) onboarding to the Freshfields **Common Data Framework (CDF)** implementation in repo `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`.
>
> **Source of truth**: This repo + `.cursor/rules/` (local, gitignored). Live Azure/Databricks values in [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]].

## What is CDF?

**Common Data Framework (CDF)** is Freshfields' enterprise data platform programme. This repository implements the **Data Landing Zone (DLZ)** — code `data-platform` — a contract-driven lakehouse on **Azure Data Factory** + **Azure Databricks** + **ADLS Gen2**.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  SRC["13 source systems"] --> ADF["Azure Data Factory"]
  ADF --> RAW

  subgraph MED ["Medallion · ADLS data-platform"]
    direction LR
    RAW["RAW"] --> BASE["BASE"] --> ENR["ENRICHED"] --> CUR["CURATED"] --> MOD["MODEL · Gold UC"]
  end

  DBX["Databricks · notebooks + Unity Catalog"]
  MED -.->|transforms| DBX

  CDF["CDF Lookup"] --> WDM["Worker Data Mart"]
  ENR --> WDM
  ENR --> CM["Canonical Model"]

  EGR["Egress pipelines"]
  CUR --> EGR
  ENR --> EGR
  EGR --> EXT["Benevity · Milana · PredictX · Cosmos DB"]
```

*Ingestion lands RAW via ADF; Databricks promotes every medallion stage; data products build in ENRICHED/CURATED; egress pushes outbound feeds only.*

## Read this first (agent checklist)

1. [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/platform-architecture]] — how ingestion, curation, and egress connect
2. [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/repo-structure]] — where to edit code vs metadata
3. [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/data-contracts-and-metadata]] — contracts drive ADF + notebooks
4. [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/agent-toolkit-and-rules]] — MCP, CLI, auth, constraints

# Data Platform DLZ

> **Audience**: Cursor agents (and humans) onboarding to the Freshfields **Common Data Framework (CDF)** implementation in repo `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`.
>
> **Source of truth**: This repo + `.cursor/rules/` (local, gitignored). Live Azure/Databricks values in [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]].

## What is CDF?

**Common Data Framework (CDF)** is Freshfields' enterprise data platform programme. This repository implements the **Data Landing Zone (DLZ)** — code `data-platform` — a contract-driven lakehouse on **Azure Data Factory** + **Azure Databricks** + **ADLS Gen2**.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  SRC["13 source systems"] --> ADF["Azure Data Factory"]
  ADF --> RAW

  subgraph MED ["Medallion · ADLS data-platform"]
    direction LR
    RAW["RAW"] --> BASE["BASE"] --> ENR["ENRICHED"] --> CUR["CURATED"] --> MOD["MODEL · Gold UC"]
  end

  DBX["Databricks · notebooks + Unity Catalog"]
  MED -.->|transforms| DBX

  CDF["CDF Lookup"] --> WDM["Worker Data Mart"]
  ENR --> WDM
  ENR --> CM["Canonical Model"]

  EGR["Egress pipelines"]
  CUR --> EGR
  ENR --> EGR
  EGR --> EXT["Benevity · Milana · PredictX · Cosmos DB"]
```

*Ingestion lands RAW via ADF; Databricks promotes every medallion stage; data products build in ENRICHED/CURATED; egress pushes outbound feeds only.*

# Data Platform DLZ

> **Audience**: Cursor agents (and humans) onboarding to the Freshfields **Common Data Framework (CDF)** implementation in repo `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`.
>
> **Source of truth**: This repo + `.cursor/rules/` (local, gitignored). Live Azure/Databricks values in [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]].

## What is CDF?

**Common Data Framework (CDF)** is Freshfields' enterprise data platform programme. This repository implements the **Data Landing Zone (DLZ)** — code `data-platform` — a contract-driven lakehouse on **Azure Data Factory** + **Azure Databricks** + **ADLS Gen2**.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  SRC["13 source systems"] --> ADF["Azure Data Factory"]
  ADF --> RAW

  subgraph MED ["Medallion · ADLS data-platform"]
    direction LR
    RAW["RAW"] --> BASE["BASE"] --> ENR["ENRICHED"] --> CUR["CURATED"] --> MOD["MODEL · Gold UC"]
  end

  DBX["Databricks · notebooks + Unity Catalog"]
  MED -.->|transforms| DBX

  CDF["CDF Lookup"] --> WDM["Worker Data Mart"]
  ENR --> WDM
  ENR --> CM["Canonical Model"]

  EGR["Egress pipelines"]
  CUR --> EGR
  ENR --> EGR
  EGR --> EXT["Benevity · Milana · PredictX · Cosmos DB"]
```

*Ingestion lands RAW via ADF; Databricks promotes every medallion stage; data products build in ENRICHED/CURATED; egress pushes outbound feeds only.*

frontmatter.title: Benevity Integration
frontmatter.summary: Benevity is an egress-only integration: Workday BASE → ENRICHED BenevityEmployee → benevity.employee view → Task - Egress Benevity CSV to SFTP. Benevity is NOT an ingestion source.

arguments.content:
# Benevity Integration

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | Products: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] | Egress: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]]

## Summary

**Benevity is egress-only.** It does not appear in the 12 ingestion source systems ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/source-systems]]). Employee data originates in **Workday**, is reshaped in the CDF into a Benevity-specific feed, and pushed out as a daily-stamped CSV. Two contracts implement this: the `Benevity` data product (ENRICHED dataset) and the `BenevityEgress` product (outbound wiring).

```
Workday RaaS → RAW → BASE (silver workday.*)
  → ENRICHED BenevityEmployee (gold benevity schema)
    → view benevity.employee
      → Task - Egress Benevity (ADF) → FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv → SFTP (SHIR file server)
```

## Contracts

| Contract | Path | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `Benevity` product | `metadata/contracts/data-products/Benevity/_dataProduct.json` | `Databricks Hive Database` — gold `benevity` schema |
| `BenevityEmployee` entity | `.../Benevity/BenevityEmployee.json` | `type: "Enriched Dataset"`, `curationStages: []` — ENRICHED-only, 18 columns, PK `EmployeeId`, natural key `Email` |
| `BenevityEgress` product | `metadata/contracts/data-products/BenevityEgress/_dataProduct.json` | `targetType: "Benevity"` + Key Vault secret names |
| `BenevityEmployeeEgress` entity | `.../BenevityEgress/BenevityEmployeeEgress.json` | `egressDetails`: Full method, source query, file name/type |
| Source CSVs | `metadata/contracts/scripts/data-products/Benevity-*.csv`, `BenevityEgress-*.csv` | Codegen inputs — see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]] |

### Dependencies (from `BenevityEmployee.json`)

| BASE entity (silver `workday.*`) | Type | Used for |
|---|---|---|
| `workday_person` | Hard | Core employee attributes (main FROM) |
| `workday_person_inter_office_assignment` | Hard | Secondment location and end date |
| `workday_office` | Soft | Office ID / country for assignments |
| `workday_colleague` | Soft | `Worker_Type = 'Colleague'` + active-status filter |

`BenevityEmployeeEgress` has one Hard dependency: `ENRICHED / Benevity / BenevityEmployee` — so egress only runs after the enriched table refreshes (dependency queue → Event Grid, see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]]).

## ENRICHED notebook

`transformation/src/databricks/3 - ENRICHED/Benevity/Enriched - Benevity - Enriched Dataset - Benevity Employee.py` — standard Adatis Framework pattern ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/curation-pipeline]]), single-source-ish Enriched Dataset with **hand-edited business SQL** (autogen `replaceIfExists=False` preserves it).

Business rules baked into the SQL:

- **LocalizedExperience CTE** — hard-coded `VALUES` mapping employee country → Benevity programme code/country (mapping *requested and provided by Benevity*): e.g. Germany/Austria/NL → `HdS`/Germany, UK/UAE → `CG`/UK, US/APAC → `AOGF`/United States, France → `FPS`/France.
- **Exclusions** — rows dropped for Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and UAE outside Dubai; also any country not in the mapping (`LocalizedExperienceCode != 'Unknown'`).
- **Filters** — `workday_colleague.Worker_Type = 'Colleague'` and `Active_Status_Flag = 1`.
- **Dedup** — `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Colleague_ID ORDER BY Hire_Date DESC)`, keep row 1.
- **MiddleInitial heuristic** — keep `Middle_Name` only if it is 1 char or ≤5 chars containing a dot.

Outputs:

| Object | Value |
|---|---|
| Delta path | `ENRICHED/General/Benevity/BenevityEmployee/delta/` |
| Gold table | `benevity.enriched_benevity_benevityemployee` (overwrite + overwriteSchema) |
| Gold view | `benevity.employee` — column-pruned, `ORDER BY INT(EmployeeId)`; **this view is the egress contract surface** |

The `benevity` gold schema is created by `1 - Post Deployment/Create Metastore Schemas.py` (see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution]]).

## Egress leg (ADF)

`Child - Egress Data` switches on `egressConnectionDetails.targetType` → case `"Benevity"` → **`Task - Egress Benevity`** (`ingestion/src/data-factory/pipeline/`).

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Source | `AzureDatabricksDeltaLakeSource`, query from `egressDetails.sourceQueryTemplate` = `SELECT * FROM {{Catalog}}.benevity.employee` (catalog token replaced at runtime) |
| Staging | `enableStaging: true` via ADLS `data-platform/TEMP/Benevity/` |
| Sink | `Benevity_Target` dataset → `FilesystemSHIR_Source` linked service (file server over SHIR), `DelimitedTextSink`, MergeFiles, quoted CSV with header |
| File name | `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` (UTC date stamp appended by pipeline expression) |
| Egress method | `Full` (no watermark/incremental, unlike Milana which supports incremental + deletes) |
| Credentials | Key Vault `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-kv`: `HostFor-Benevity`, `UsernameFor-Benevity`, `PasswordTo-Benevity` — secret *names* in the contract, values seeded by `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml` from variable group `TargetSystems.Benevity.*` |

## Agent checklist for Benevity changes

1. **Column change** → update both CSVs + both entity contracts (`BenevityEmployee` and `BenevityEmployeeEgress` schemas must stay in sync) and the notebook SQL + `benevity.employee` view definition.
2. **Mapping change** (localized experience, exclusions) → edit the notebook SQL only; the mapping is hard-coded, not contract-driven.
3. **Connection change** → `BenevityEgress/_dataProduct.json` secret names + Key Vault values via `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml`.
4. **Do not** look for a Benevity ingestion contract — none exists; the upstream is Workday.

# Benevity Integration

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | Products: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] | Egress: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]]

**Benevity is egress-only.** It does not appear in the 12 ingestion source systems ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/source-systems]]). Employee data originates in **Workday**, is reshaped in the CDF into a Benevity-specific feed, and pushed out as a daily-stamped CSV. Two contracts implement this: the `Benevity` data product (ENRICHED dataset) and the `BenevityEgress` product (outbound wiring).

```
Workday RaaS → RAW → BASE (silver workday.*)
  → ENRICHED BenevityEmployee (gold benevity schema)
    → view benevity.employee
      → Task - Egress Benevity (ADF) → FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv → SFTP (SHIR file server)
```

## Contracts

| Contract | Path | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `Benevity` product | `metadata/contracts/data-products/Benevity/_dataProduct.json` | `Databricks Hive Database` — gold `benevity` schema |
| `BenevityEmployee` entity | `.../Benevity/BenevityEmployee.json` | `type: "Enriched Dataset"`, `curationStages: []` — ENRICHED-only, 18 columns, PK `EmployeeId`, natural key `Email` |
| `BenevityEgress` product | `metadata/contracts/data-products/BenevityEgress/_dataProduct.json` | `targetType: "Benevity"` + Key Vault secret names |
| `BenevityEmployeeEgress` entity | `.../BenevityEgress/BenevityEmployeeEgress.json` | `egressDetails`: Full method, source query, file name/type |
| Source CSVs | `metadata/contracts/scripts/data-products/Benevity-*.csv`, `BenevityEgress-*.csv` | Codegen inputs — see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]] |

### Dependencies (from `BenevityEmployee.json`)

| BASE entity (silver `workday.*`) | Type | Used for |
|---|---|---|
| `workday_person` | Hard | Core employee attributes (main FROM) |
| `workday_person_inter_office_assignment` | Hard | Secondment location and end date |
| `workday_office` | Soft | Office ID / country for assignments |
| `workday_colleague` | Soft | `Worker_Type = 'Colleague'` + active-status filter |

`BenevityEmployeeEgress` has one Hard dependency: `ENRICHED / Benevity / BenevityEmployee` — so egress only runs after the enriched table refreshes (dependency queue → Event Grid, see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]]).

## ENRICHED notebook

`transformation/src/databricks/3 - ENRICHED/Benevity/Enriched - Benevity - Enriched Dataset - Benevity Employee.py` — standard Adatis Framework pattern ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/curation-pipeline]]), single-source-ish Enriched Dataset with **hand-edited business SQL** (autogen `replaceIfExists=False` preserves it).

Business rules baked into the SQL:

- **LocalizedExperience CTE** — hard-coded `VALUES` mapping employee country → Benevity programme code/country (mapping *requested and provided by Benevity*): e.g. Germany/Austria/NL → `HdS`/Germany, UK/UAE → `CG`/UK, US/APAC → `AOGF`/United States, France → `FPS`/France.
- **Exclusions** — rows dropped for Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and UAE outside Dubai; also any country not in the mapping (`LocalizedExperienceCode != 'Unknown'`).
- **Filters** — `workday_colleague.Worker_Type = 'Colleague'` and `Active_Status_Flag = 1`.
- **Dedup** — `ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Colleague_ID ORDER BY Hire_Date DESC)`, keep row 1.
- **MiddleInitial heuristic** — keep `Middle_Name` only if it is 1 char or ≤5 chars containing a dot.

Outputs:

| Object | Value |
|---|---|
| Delta path | `ENRICHED/General/Benevity/BenevityEmployee/delta/` |
| Gold table | `benevity.enriched_benevity_benevityemployee` (overwrite + overwriteSchema) |
| Gold view | `benevity.employee` — column-pruned, `ORDER BY INT(EmployeeId)`; **this view is the egress contract surface** |

`Child - Egress Data` switches on `egressConnectionDetails.targetType` → case `"Benevity"` → **`Task - Egress Benevity`** (`ingestion/src/data-factory/pipeline/`).

| File name | `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` (UTC date stamp appended by pipeline expression) |
| Egress method | `Full` (no watermark/incremental, unlike Milana which supports incremental + deletes) |
| Credentials | Key Vault `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-kv`: `HostFor-Benevity`, `UsernameFor-Benevity`, `PasswordTo-Benevity` — secret *names* in the contract, values seeded by `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml` from variable group `TargetSystems.Benevity.*` |

2. **Mapping change** (localized experience, exclusions) → edit the notebook SQL only; the mapping is hard-coded, not contract-driven.

frontmatter.title: Egress Pipeline
frontmatter.summary: Outbound DLZ data flow: Orchestrator - Egress → Parent/Child - Egress Data → targetType switch → Task - Egress <Target>. Contract egressDetails drive everything; five targets today.

arguments.content:
# Egress Pipeline

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | Orchestration: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]]

## Summary

Egress is the **outbound leg** of the DLZ: contract-driven ADF pipelines that read curated/enriched gold objects from Databricks and push them to external systems. It mirrors the ingestion pattern — same process instance / process queue machinery, same Azure Function metadata layer — but fans out on `egressConnectionDetails.targetType` instead of source type.

## Pipeline chain (`ingestion/src/data-factory/pipeline/`)

```
Orchestrator - Egress (param: DataProductCode)
  → CreateProcessInstance → PopulateProcessQueue (EgressDataProductCode)
  → Parent - Egress Data (ProcessInstanceId)
      → GetEgressEntityList → ForEach (parallel, batch 10)
          → Child - Egress Data (ProcessQueueId)
              → GetProcessQueueEntry → GetDataProduct → GetDataProductEntity
              → Switch on egressConnectionDetails.targetType
                  → Task - Egress <Target>
              → UpdateProcessQueueEntry (Successful | Failed)
```

All metadata calls go through the **`AzureFunction_Metadata`** linked service; parameters like source query, file name, and secret names come from the entity's `egressDetails` and the product's `egressConnectionDetails` — the task pipelines contain no hard-coded connection values.

## Targets (switch cases in `Child - Egress Data`)

| targetType | Task pipeline | Sink | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Milana` | Task - Egress Milana | SFTP (private key + passphrase secrets) | Supports **incremental** egress: `incrementalEgressDetails` (`maxWatermarkQuery`, `includeDeletes`) |
| `Cosmos DB` | Task - Egress Cosmos DB | Cosmos container/database | `sourceQueryTemplate` used as wildcard path |
| `Predict X` | Task - Egress Predict X | File server (username/password) | Full export |
| `Benevity` | Task - Egress Benevity | File server via SHIR (username/password) | Full export — deep dive: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| (Travel Operations) | Task - Egress Travel Operations | File server | Case exists in pipeline |
| *default* | Fail activity | — | "No handler has been configured for the supplied source type code" |

Valid `targetType` values are constrained by the schema enum in `framework/contracts/schemas/dataProduct.json`: `Azure Blob Storage`, `Milana`, `Cosmos DB`, `SnapLogic`, `Predict X`, `Benevity`.

## Common Task pipeline anatomy (file-based targets)

1. **Update Catalog in Source Query Template** — replaces the `{{Catalog}}` token with the environment's gold catalog.
2. **Copy activity** — `AzureDatabricksDeltaLakeSource` (SQL query against gold view) → delimited-text sink; `enableStaging: true` through ADLS `data-platform/TEMP/<Target>/`.
3. **File naming** — `@concat(TargetFileName, '_', formatDateTime(utcNow(),'yyyyMMdd'), '.', TargetFileType)`.
4. **Credentials** — datasets take *secret names* as parameters; linked service (`FilesystemSHIR_Source` for file targets) resolves them from Key Vault `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-kv` at runtime. Secret values are seeded by `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml` from Azure DevOps variable groups (`TargetSystems.<Target>.*`).

## How egress is triggered

| Mechanism | Trigger file | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Schedule** | `trigger/Milana Egress.json`, `Predict X Egress.json`, `Travel Operations Egress.json` | Call `Orchestrator - Egress` with the product code (Milana's is `runtimeState: Stopped` in repo) |
| **Event Grid dependency** | `trigger/Event Grid - Dependency Triggered - Egress.json` | Fires `Child - Egress Data` directly on `DependencyTriggered` events where `CurationStageCode = EGRESS` — i.e. when the upstream ENRICHED/CURATED entity completes |
| **Manual** | — | Run `Orchestrator - Egress` with `DataProductCode` (e.g. `BenevityEgress`) |

**There is no scheduled Benevity trigger in the repo** — Benevity egress runs via the Event Grid dependency path (its `BenevityEmployeeEgress` contract declares a Hard dependency on ENRICHED `Benevity/BenevityEmployee`) or a manual orchestrator run. ^[inferred]

The Event Grid trigger scope points at topic `fbd-d-euw-data-dmlz-evgt` in the **IaC subscription** (`ec181bdf-...`) — artifact value only, per the usual rule ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]]).

## Agent checklist

- **New egress target** → new `Task - Egress <X>` pipeline + new switch case in `Child - Egress Data` + schema enum addition in `framework/contracts/schemas/dataProduct.json` (framework file — vendor boundary, flag to user) + `<X>Egress` product contract + Key Vault secrets in `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml`.
- **New entity on existing target** → entity contract with `egressDetails` + dependency on the source ENRICHED/CURATED entity; no ADF change needed.
- **Egress query** always targets a **gold view/table** — keep the view (e.g. `benevity.employee`) as the stable contract surface rather than pointing at `enriched_*` tables.

## Related

- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] — worked example end to end
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]] — ingestion-side counterpart, process queue
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] — egress product catalogue

# Data Products

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]]

## Summary

**14 data products** defined in `metadata/contracts/data-products/`. Most are Databricks Hive/Delta databases; some are **Egress** targets only.

## Analytical products (Databricks)

| Code | Name | Primary consumers / purpose |
|------|------|----------------------------|
| `WorkerDataMart` | Worker Data Mart | **Core mart** — facts/dims for time, matters, invoices, pitches, workers |
| `CDFLookup` | CDF Lookup | **Reference data** — departments, offices, calendars, hierarchies → see dedicated note |
| `CanonicalModel` | Canonical Model | Enterprise canonical entities (Client, Matter, Department, …) |
| `PartnerPortal` | Partner Portal | Partner-facing matter/WIP/invoice views |
| `DataQuality` | Data Quality | DQX comparisons and quality datasets |
| `DataOps` | Data Ops | Operational metadata (e.g. Collibra job execution) |
| `Milana` | Milana | Internal analytics product |
| `PredictX` | Predict X | Predictive / ML feature product |
| `Benevity` | Benevity | Workday-sourced employee feed shaped for Benevity → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| `API` | API | API-serving layer metadata |

## Egress products

Outbound wiring — architecture: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].

| Code | Name | Direction |
|------|------|-----------|
| `MilanaEgress` | Milana Egress | DLZ → Milana (SFTP; supports incremental + deletes) |
| `PredictXEgress` | Predict X Egress | DLZ → PredictX (file server) |
| `BenevityEgress` | Benevity Egress | DLZ → Benevity (SHIR file server, daily-stamped CSV) → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| `CosmosDbEgress` | Cosmos DB Egress | DLZ → Cosmos (e.g. Intranet KPI) |

**Egress product pattern**: the `<X>` analytical product owns the gold schema/view (e.g. `benevity.employee`); the `<X>Egress` product holds `egressConnectionDetails` (targetType + Key Vault secret *names*) and per-entity `egressDetails` (source query template, file name/type, egress method). The egress entity declares a Hard dependency on the ENRICHED entity so egress only runs after refresh.

## Dependency hub

**Worker Data Mart** is the largest downstream consumer. It references **CDF Lookup** for:

- `DepartmentName`, `ReportingOfficeName`, `OfficeCrosswalk`
- `InvoiceType`, `TimecardHierarchy`, `WorkCalendar`

Check `dependencies` / source comments in WDM enriched notebooks.

## Notebook locations

| Product | Folder |
|---------|--------|
| Worker Data Mart | `3 - ENRICHED/Worker Data Mart/` |
| CDF Lookup | `3 - ENRICHED/CDF Lookup/` |
| Partner Portal | `3 - ENRICHED/Partner Portal/` |
| Data Quality | `3 - ENRICHED/Data Quality/` |
| Benevity | `3 - ENRICHED/Benevity/` |
| Canonical Model | `4 - CURATED/Canonical Model/` |

## Agent tips

- Product `code` in contracts uses **PascalCase** (`CDFLookup`); folder names may use spaces (`CDF Lookup`).
- Entity `type` drives orchestration: Dimension, Fact, Enriched Dataset, Canonical, Egress.
- Egress entities have `egressDetails` in schema — wired through **Parent - Egress Data** → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].
- **Egress targets are not source systems** — e.g. Benevity never feeds data *in*; its upstream is Workday.


## BigHand (added 2026-06-30)

**`BigHand`** — a new `Databricks Hive Database` data product wrapping the BigHand resource-management API source. Created as an **ENRICHED-only** product: four single-source `Enriched Dataset` entities mirroring the BASE `bighand_*` tables with normalised types and pruned columns (no joins).

| Entity (code) | BASE source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Offices` | `bighand.bighand_offices` | PK `officeId`; working-day flags as `bit` |
| `WorkingArrangements` | `bighand.bighand_workingarrangements` | PK `employeeId`; `effectiveStartDate`→`date`; per-day hours `decimal(16,2)` |
| `Forecasts` | `bighand.bighand_forecasts` | `commitmentDate`→`date`; `commitmentHours` decimal |
| `Qualifications` | `bighand.bighand_qualifications` | country/region/court code+name strings |

- Contracts: `metadata/contracts/data-products/BigHand/` (`_dataProduct.json` + 4 entities). Notebooks: `3 - ENRICHED/BigHand/`. Outputs to `silver.temp.enriched_bighand_*` (no gold table unless a CURATED stage is added later).
- Generation/wiring details: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]]; ENRICHED pattern: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/curation-pipeline]].
- This brings the analytical-product count to **11** (was 10). BigHand ingestion already existed as a source system; this adds the curated/enriched product layer over it.


## Benevity (detailed 2026-07-02)

**`Benevity`** + **`BenevityEgress`** together implement the outbound employee feed: Workday BASE → ENRICHED `BenevityEmployee` (hand-edited SQL: localized-experience mapping, country exclusions, active-colleague filter) → gold view `benevity.employee` → `Task - Egress Benevity` → `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` over SHIR. Full walkthrough: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]].

arguments.content:
# Egress Pipeline

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]] | Orchestration: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]]

## Summary

Egress is the **outbound leg** of the DLZ: contract-driven ADF pipelines that read curated/enriched gold objects from Databricks and push them to external systems. It mirrors the ingestion pattern — same process instance / process queue machinery, same Azure Function metadata layer — but fans out on `egressConnectionDetails.targetType` instead of source type.

## Pipeline chain (`ingestion/src/data-factory/pipeline/`)

```
Orchestrator - Egress (param: DataProductCode)
  → CreateProcessInstance → PopulateProcessQueue (EgressDataProductCode)
  → Parent - Egress Data (ProcessInstanceId)
      → GetEgressEntityList → ForEach (parallel, batch 10)
          → Child - Egress Data (ProcessQueueId)
              → GetProcessQueueEntry → GetDataProduct → GetDataProductEntity
              → Switch on egressConnectionDetails.targetType
                  → Task - Egress <Target>
              → UpdateProcessQueueEntry (Successful | Failed)
```

All metadata calls go through the **`AzureFunction_Metadata`** linked service; parameters like source query, file name, and secret names come from the entity's `egressDetails` and the product's `egressConnectionDetails` — the task pipelines contain no hard-coded connection values.

## Targets (switch cases in `Child - Egress Data`)

| targetType | Task pipeline | Sink | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Milana` | Task - Egress Milana | SFTP (private key + passphrase secrets) | Supports **incremental** egress: `incrementalEgressDetails` (`maxWatermarkQuery`, `includeDeletes`) |
| `Cosmos DB` | Task - Egress Cosmos DB | Cosmos container/database | `sourceQueryTemplate` used as wildcard path |
| `Predict X` | Task - Egress Predict X | File server (username/password) | Full export |
| `Benevity` | Task - Egress Benevity | File server via SHIR (username/password) | Full export — deep dive: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| (Travel Operations) | Task - Egress Travel Operations | File server | Case exists in pipeline |
| *default* | Fail activity | — | "No handler has been configured for the supplied source type code" |

Valid `targetType` values are constrained by the schema enum in `framework/contracts/schemas/dataProduct.json`: `Azure Blob Storage`, `Milana`, `Cosmos DB`, `SnapLogic`, `Predict X`, `Benevity`.

## Common Task pipeline anatomy (file-based targets)

1. **Update Catalog in Source Query Template** — replaces the `{{Catalog}}` token with the environment's gold catalog.
2. **Copy activity** — `AzureDatabricksDeltaLakeSource` (SQL query against gold view) → delimited-text sink; `enableStaging: true` through ADLS `data-platform/TEMP/<Target>/`.
3. **File naming** — `@concat(TargetFileName, '_', formatDateTime(utcNow(),'yyyyMMdd'), '.', TargetFileType)`.
4. **Credentials** — datasets take *secret names* as parameters; linked service (`FilesystemSHIR_Source` for file targets) resolves them from Key Vault `fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-kv` at runtime. Secret values are seeded by `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml` from Azure DevOps variable groups (`TargetSystems.<Target>.*`).

## How egress is triggered

| Mechanism | Trigger file | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Schedule** | `trigger/Milana Egress.json`, `Predict X Egress.json`, `Travel Operations Egress.json` | Call `Orchestrator - Egress` with the product code (Milana's is `runtimeState: Stopped` in repo) |
| **Event Grid dependency** | `trigger/Event Grid - Dependency Triggered - Egress.json` | Fires `Child - Egress Data` directly on `DependencyTriggered` events where `CurationStageCode = EGRESS` — i.e. when the upstream ENRICHED/CURATED entity completes |
| **Manual** | — | Run `Orchestrator - Egress` with `DataProductCode` (e.g. `BenevityEgress`) |

**There is no scheduled Benevity trigger in the repo** — Benevity egress runs via the Event Grid dependency path (its `BenevityEmployeeEgress` contract declares a Hard dependency on ENRICHED `Benevity/BenevityEmployee`) or a manual orchestrator run. ^[inferred]

The Event Grid trigger scope points at topic `fbd-d-euw-data-dmlz-evgt` in the **IaC subscription** (`ec181bdf-...`) — artifact value only, per the usual rule ([[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]]).

## Agent checklist

- **New egress target** → new `Task - Egress <X>` pipeline + new switch case in `Child - Egress Data` + schema enum addition in `framework/contracts/schemas/dataProduct.json` (framework file — vendor boundary, flag to user) + `<X>Egress` product contract + Key Vault secrets in `secrets/yaml/deploy.yml`.
- **New entity on existing target** → entity contract with `egressDetails` + dependency on the source ENRICHED/CURATED entity; no ADF change needed.
- **Egress query** always targets a **gold view/table** — keep the view (e.g. `benevity.employee`) as the stable contract surface rather than pointing at `enriched_*` tables.

## Related

- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] — worked example end to end
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]] — ingestion-side counterpart, process queue
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] — egress product catalogue

**`BigHand`** — a new `Databricks Hive Database` data product wrapping the BigHand resource-management API source. Created as an **ENRICHED-only** product: four single-source `Enriched Dataset` entities mirroring the BASE `bighand_*` tables with normalised types and pruned columns (no joins).

| Entity (code) | BASE source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Offices` | `bighand.bighand_offices` | PK `officeId`; working-day flags as `bit` |
| `WorkingArrangements` | `bighand.bighand_workingarrangements` | PK `employeeId`; `effectiveStartDate`→`date`; per-day hours `decimal(16,2)` |
| `Forecasts` | `bighand.bighand_forecasts` | `commitmentDate`→`date`; `commitmentHours` decimal |
| `Qualifications` | `bighand.bighand_qualifications` | country/region/court code+name strings |

- Contracts: `metadata/contracts/data-products/BigHand/` (`_dataProduct.json` + 4 entities). Notebooks: `3 - ENRICHED/BigHand/`. Outputs to `silver.temp.enriched_bighand_*` (no gold table unless a CURATED stage is added later).
- Generation/wiring details: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]]; ENRICHED pattern: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/curation-pipeline]].
- This brings the analytical-product count to **11** (was 10). BigHand ingestion already existed as a source system; this adds the curated/enriched product layer over it.


## Benevity (detailed 2026-07-02)

**`Benevity`** + **`BenevityEgress`** together implement the outbound employee feed: Workday BASE → ENRICHED `BenevityEmployee` (hand-edited SQL: localized-experience mapping, country exclusions, active-colleague filter) → gold view `benevity.employee` → `Task - Egress Benevity` → `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` over SHIR. Full walkthrough: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]].

# Data Products

[[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz|← Index]]

## Summary

**14 data products** defined in `metadata/contracts/data-products/`. Most are Databricks Hive/Delta databases; some are **Egress** targets only.

## Analytical products (Databricks)

| Code | Name | Primary consumers / purpose |
|------|------|----------------------------|
| `WorkerDataMart` | Worker Data Mart | **Core mart** — facts/dims for time, matters, invoices, pitches, workers |
| `CDFLookup` | CDF Lookup | **Reference data** — departments, offices, calendars, hierarchies → see dedicated note |
| `CanonicalModel` | Canonical Model | Enterprise canonical entities (Client, Matter, Department, …) |
| `PartnerPortal` | Partner Portal | Partner-facing matter/WIP/invoice views |
| `DataQuality` | Data Quality | DQX comparisons and quality datasets |
| `DataOps` | Data Ops | Operational metadata (e.g. Collibra job execution) |
| `Milana` | Milana | Internal analytics product |
| `PredictX` | Predict X | Predictive / ML feature product |
| `Benevity` | Benevity | Workday-sourced employee feed shaped for Benevity → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| `API` | API | API-serving layer metadata |

## Egress products

Outbound wiring — architecture: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].

| Code | Name | Direction |
|------|------|-----------|
| `MilanaEgress` | Milana Egress | DLZ → Milana (SFTP; supports incremental + deletes) |
| `PredictXEgress` | Predict X Egress | DLZ → PredictX (file server) |
| `BenevityEgress` | Benevity Egress | DLZ → Benevity (SHIR file server, daily-stamped CSV) → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] |
| `CosmosDbEgress` | Cosmos DB Egress | DLZ → Cosmos (e.g. Intranet KPI) |

**Egress product pattern**: the `<X>` analytical product owns the gold schema/view (e.g. `benevity.employee`); the `<X>Egress` product holds `egressConnectionDetails` (targetType + Key Vault secret *names*) and per-entity `egressDetails` (source query template, file name/type, egress method). The egress entity declares a Hard dependency on the ENRICHED entity so egress only runs after refresh.

## Dependency hub

**Worker Data Mart** is the largest downstream consumer. It references **CDF Lookup** for:

- `DepartmentName`, `ReportingOfficeName`, `OfficeCrosswalk`
- `InvoiceType`, `TimecardHierarchy`, `WorkCalendar`

Check `dependencies` / source comments in WDM enriched notebooks.

## Notebook locations

| Product | Folder |
|---------|--------|
| Worker Data Mart | `3 - ENRICHED/Worker Data Mart/` |
| CDF Lookup | `3 - ENRICHED/CDF Lookup/` |
| Partner Portal | `3 - ENRICHED/Partner Portal/` |
| Data Quality | `3 - ENRICHED/Data Quality/` |
| Benevity | `3 - ENRICHED/Benevity/` |
| Canonical Model | `4 - CURATED/Canonical Model/` |

## Agent tips

- Product `code` in contracts uses **PascalCase** (`CDFLookup`); folder names may use spaces (`CDF Lookup`).
- Entity `type` drives orchestration: Dimension, Fact, Enriched Dataset, Canonical, Egress.
- Egress entities have `egressDetails` in schema — wired through **Parent - Egress Data** → [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].
- **Egress targets are not source systems** — e.g. Benevity never feeds data *in*; its upstream is Workday.

| `Forecasts` | `bighand.bighand_forecasts` | `commitmentDate`→`date`; `commitmentHours` decimal |
| `Qualifications` | `bighand.bighand_qualifications` | country/region/court code+name strings |

- Contracts: `metadata/contracts/data-products/BigHand/` (`_dataProduct.json` + 4 entities). Notebooks: `3 - ENRICHED/BigHand/`. Outputs to `silver.temp.enriched_bighand_*` (no gold table unless a CURATED stage is added later).
- Generation/wiring details: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]]; ENRICHED pattern: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/curation-pipeline]].
- This brings the analytical-product count to **11** (was 10). BigHand ingestion already existed as a source system; this adds the curated/enriched product layer over it.

Full walkthrough: [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]].

arguments.content:
## Egress targets are NOT source systems (clarified 2026-07-02)

External systems such as **Benevity**, **Milana**, and **Predict X** appear in contracts and gold schemas but **never feed data in** — they are outbound targets only. If asked "how does Benevity data get in", the answer is: it doesn't; the Benevity feed is *built from Workday* (`workday_person`, `workday_person_inter_office_assignment`, `workday_office`, `workday_colleague`) and pushed out. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] and [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].

**Agent note**: a gold schema named after an external system (e.g. `benevity`) usually holds the *outbound* dataset for that system, not ingested data from it.

## Egress targets are NOT source systems (clarified 2026-07-02)

External systems such as **Benevity**, **Milana**, and **Predict X** appear in contracts and gold schemas but **never feed data in** — they are outbound targets only. If asked "how does Benevity data get in", the answer is: it doesn't; the Benevity feed is *built from Workday* (`workday_person`, `workday_person_inter_office_assignment`, `workday_office`, `workday_colleague`) and pushed out. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] and [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].

**Agent note**: a gold schema named after an external system (e.g. `benevity`) usually holds the *outbound* dataset for that system, not ingested data from it.

## Egress detail (expanded 2026-07-02)

The egress side has its own full chain mirroring ingestion — now documented in [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]]:

- **Orchestrator - Egress** (param `DataProductCode`) → `CreateProcessInstance` → `PopulateProcessQueue` (with `EgressDataProductCode`) → **Parent - Egress Data**.
- **Parent - Egress Data** calls `GetEgressEntityList` and fans out (parallel, batch 10) to **Child - Egress Data** per `ProcessQueueId`.
- **Child - Egress Data** fetches product + entity metadata via `GetDataProduct` / `GetDataProductEntity`, then a **Switch on `egressConnectionDetails.targetType`** routes to `Task - Egress Milana | Cosmos DB | Predict X | Benevity | Travel Operations`; queue entry marked Successful/Failed via `UpdateProcessQueueEntry`.
- Scheduled triggers exist for Milana / Predict X / Travel Operations (calling Orchestrator - Egress); the **Event Grid - Dependency Triggered - Egress** trigger fires Child - Egress Data directly when `CurationStageCode = EGRESS` dependencies fire. Benevity has no scheduled trigger — dependency-driven. ^[inferred]

- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] — Egress-only Benevity feed: Workday → ENRICHED → benevity.employee → daily CSV
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/cdf-lookup]] — ENRICHED-only reference data product (deep dive)
- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]] — CSV → contracts (PowerShell) → notebooks (Python adatis_autogen)

- [[entities/azure-service-bus]] — Service Bus instances in Freshfields Azure

- [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/cursor-plugins-and-tooling-status]] — Plugin/MCP health and ec181bdf vs 8c66c246 subscription bottom-out

*No pages yet.*

## Meta

- [[_meta/migration-map]] — Legacy path → current wiki location
- [[_archives/README]] — Archive policy (pre-migration duplicates removed 2026-06-30)

# Hot Cache

## Recent Activity

- [2026-07-02] Session capture: **Benevity flow + egress architecture** — answered "how does Benevity data get into the CDF": it doesn't; Benevity is **egress-only** (Workday → ENRICHED `BenevityEmployee` → `benevity.employee` view → `Task - Egress Benevity` → `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` over SHIR). New pages [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] and [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]]; updates to [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]], [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/source-systems]], [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]].
- [2026-06-30] Session capture: **BASE metastore deploy failure** — the `Create BASE Metastore Tables` step failed with PowerShell `Job  failed`; real cause was the `handle_schema_change()` **destructive-change guard** on `bighand.bighand_qualifications` (STRING→TIMESTAMP + dropped prefixed columns). New page [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution]]; runner gotcha added to [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/deployment-and-ci-cd]]. Remediated with `DROP TABLE ... PURGE` (external Delta table).
- [2026-06-30] Session capture: **Contract generation chain** — CSV is the *source*, contracts are generated by PowerShell, then the Python `adatis_autogen` generates the Databricks notebooks. New page [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]]. Added **BigHand** ENRICHED data product (4 single-source Enriched Datasets).
- [2026-06-25] Session capture: **BaseURL deployment routing** — contracts hold secret *names*, not URLs; Secrets vs Ingestion pipelines; see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/deployment-and-ci-cd]]
- [2026-06-24] Vault consolidated — living wiki under Karpathy taxonomy

## Active Threads

- BigHand ENRICHED build: contracts + notebooks done (steps 1–3); step 4 (regenerate metastore/script artifacts) planned in repo `.cursor/plans/bighand_step4_regenerate_artifacts.plan.md`
- Data Platform DLZ onboarding via [[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz]]

## Key Takeaways

- **Egress targets are not source systems** — Benevity/Milana/PredictX gold schemas hold *outbound* datasets. The `<X>` product owns the gold view; the `<X>Egress` product holds `egressConnectionDetails` (KV secret names) + `egressDetails` (query template, file name). See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].
- **Benevity business rules live in hand-edited notebook SQL**, not contracts: localized-experience country mapping (provided by Benevity), Bahrain/Saudi/non-Dubai-UAE exclusions, active-colleague filter. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]].
- **`Create BASE/CURATED Metastore Tables` will FAIL the deploy on destructive schema changes** (type changes, column drops) via `handle_schema_change(..., drop_columns=False)`. Additive columns apply automatically. Fix = rebuild the table (`7 - Manual Data Changes/Rebuild Delta Table Template.py`, or `DROP TABLE ... PURGE` then redeploy + re-ingest). See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution]].
- **The CICD PowerShell runner hides the real error** — `Run-DatabricksJob.ps1` throws `Job  failed` (blank id, no message). Get the true cause from Databricks: `databricks jobs list-runs` → `get-run-output <run_id> --profile dlz-dev`.
- **BASE tables are EXTERNAL Delta** on ADLS (`BASE/General/<source>/<entity>/delta`) with deletion vectors — a plain `DROP TABLE` leaves files on disk; use `PURGE`.
- **"CDF" is overloaded**: Common Data Framework (programme) vs Change Data Feed (Delta CDC on gold `canonicalmodel.curated_*`) vs CDF Lookup (reference product).
- **Codegen direction is CSV → contract JSON → notebooks.** Don't assume CSVs are generated from contracts. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]].
- ENRICHED notebooks autogen with `replaceIfExists=False` (hand edits preserved); `Create Metastore Schemas` with `=True` (always regenerated; silver+gold schema lists derive from contracts).
- Silver BASE table name = `<sourceSystemCode>.<entityCode>` (e.g. `bighand.bighand_offices`), not the source `TABLE_NAME` (`bh_Offices`).
- Contract type enum has `bit` (no `boolean`); normalise `nvarchar`→`varchar`, `datetime`→`date` (`TO_DATE`).
- `adatis_autogen/main.py` is Windows/CI-path-coupled — likely not runnable as-is on macOS; never patch `framework/`.

## Flagged Contradictions

*None.*

# Hot Cache

## Recent Activity

- [2026-07-02] Session capture: **Benevity flow + egress architecture** — answered "how does Benevity data get into the CDF": it doesn't; Benevity is **egress-only** (Workday → ENRICHED `BenevityEmployee` → `benevity.employee` view → `Task - Egress Benevity` → `FBD_Benevity_<yyyyMMdd>.csv` over SHIR). New pages [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]] and [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]]; updates to [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]], [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/source-systems]], [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/orchestration-and-adf]].
- [2026-06-30] Session capture: **BASE metastore deploy failure** — the `Create BASE Metastore Tables` step failed with PowerShell `Job  failed`; real cause was the `handle_schema_change()` **destructive-change guard** on `bighand.bighand_qualifications` (STRING→TIMESTAMP + dropped prefixed columns). New page [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution]]; runner gotcha added to [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/deployment-and-ci-cd]]. Remediated with `DROP TABLE ... PURGE` (external Delta table).
- [2026-06-30] Session capture: **Contract generation chain** — CSV is the *source*, contracts are generated by PowerShell, then the Python `adatis_autogen` generates the Databricks notebooks. New page [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]]. Added **BigHand** ENRICHED data product (4 single-source Enriched Datasets).
- [2026-06-25] Session capture: **BaseURL deployment routing** — contracts hold secret *names*, not URLs; Secrets vs Ingestion pipelines; see [[projects/data-platform-dlz/skills/deployment-and-ci-cd]]
- [2026-06-24] Vault consolidated — living wiki under Karpathy taxonomy

## Active Threads

- BigHand ENRICHED build: contracts + notebooks done (steps 1–3); step 4 (regenerate metastore/script artifacts) planned in repo `.cursor/plans/bighand_step4_regenerate_artifacts.plan.md`
- Data Platform DLZ onboarding via [[projects/data-platform-dlz/data-platform-dlz]]

## Key Takeaways

- **Egress targets are not source systems** — Benevity/Milana/PredictX gold schemas hold *outbound* datasets. The `<X>` product owns the gold view; the `<X>Egress` product holds `egressConnectionDetails` (KV secret names) + `egressDetails` (query template, file name). See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]].
- **Benevity business rules live in hand-edited notebook SQL**, not contracts: localized-experience country mapping (provided by Benevity), Bahrain/Saudi/non-Dubai-UAE exclusions, active-colleague filter. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/benevity-integration]].
- **`Create BASE/CURATED Metastore Tables` will FAIL the deploy on destructive schema changes** (type changes, column drops) via `handle_schema_change(..., drop_columns=False)`. Additive columns apply automatically. Fix = rebuild the table (`7 - Manual Data Changes/Rebuild Delta Table Template.py`, or `DROP TABLE ... PURGE` then redeploy + re-ingest). See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution]].
- **The CICD PowerShell runner hides the real error** — `Run-DatabricksJob.ps1` throws `Job  failed` (blank id, no message). Get the true cause from Databricks: `databricks jobs list-runs` → `get-run-output <run_id> --profile dlz-dev`.
- **BASE tables are EXTERNAL Delta** on ADLS (`BASE/General/<source>/<entity>/delta`) with deletion vectors — a plain `DROP TABLE` leaves files on disk; use `PURGE`.
- **"CDF" is overloaded**: Common Data Framework (programme) vs Change Data Feed (Delta CDC on gold `canonicalmodel.curated_*`) vs CDF Lookup (reference product).
- **Codegen direction is CSV → contract JSON → notebooks.** Don't assume CSVs are generated from contracts. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/contract-generation-pipeline]].
- ENRICHED notebooks autogen with `replaceIfExists=False` (hand edits preserved); `Create Metastore Schemas` with `=True` (always regenerated; silver+gold schema lists derive from contracts).
- Silver BASE table name = `<sourceSystemCode>.<entityCode>` (e.g. `bighand.bighand_offices`), not the source `TABLE_NAME` (`bh_Offices`).
- Contract type enum has `bit` (no `boolean`); normalise `nvarchar`→`varchar`, `datetime`→`date` (`TO_DATE`).
- `adatis_autogen/main.py` is Windows/CI-path-coupled — likely not runnable as-is on macOS; never patch `framework/`.

## Flagged Contradictions

*None.*

# Data Platform DLZ

> **Audience**: Cursor agents (and humans) onboarding to the Freshfields **Common Data Framework (CDF)** implementation in repo `DataPlatform-DataLandingZone`.
>
> **Source of truth**: This repo + `.cursor/rules/` (local, gitignored). Live Azure/Databricks values in [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/azure-and-dlz-environment]].

## What is CDF?

**Common Data Framework (CDF)** is Freshfields' enterprise data platform programme. This repository implements the **Data Landing Zone (DLZ)** — code `data-platform` — a contract-driven lakehouse on **Azure Data Factory** + **Azure Databricks** + **ADLS Gen2**.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  subgraph sources [Source systems]
    E3E[Elite 3E]
    WD[Workday]
    SF[Salesforce]
    BH[BigHand]
    Others[10 more...]
  end
  subgraph adf [Azure Data Factory]
    MO[Main Orchestrator]
    ING[Parent - Ingest Data]
    EGR[Parent - Egress Data]
  end
  subgraph lake [ADLS data-platform container]
    RAW[RAW]
    BASE[BASE]
    ENR[ENRICHED]
    CUR[CURATED]
  end
  subgraph dbx [Databricks fbd-d-uks-data-dlz-dbw]
    NB[Notebooks /Data Platform]
    UC[Unity Catalog bronze/silver/gold]
  end
  subgraph products [Data products]
    WDM[Worker Data Mart]
    CDFL[CDF Lookup]
    PP[Partner Portal]
    CM[Canonical Model]
  end
  subgraph egress [Egress targets]
    BEN[Benevity]
    MIL[Milana]
    PX[PredictX]
    COS[Cosmos DB]
  end
  sources --> ING --> RAW
  RAW --> NB --> BASE --> ENR --> CUR
  NB --> UC
  ENR --> WDM
  CDFL --> WDM
  MO --> ING
  CUR --> EGR
  ENR --> EGR
  EGR --> egress
```

| [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/platform-architecture]] | End-to-end data flow, medallion layers, orchestration |
| [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/repo-structure]] | Top-level folders, notebook numbering, deploy paths |

| [[projects/data-platform-dlz/entities/data-products]] | All 14 published data products |

## Key terminology

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **CDF** | Common Data Framework (programme) |
| **CDF (Change Data Feed)** | Delta CDC feature; enabled on gold `canonicalmodel.curated_*` — **not** the programme. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/metastore-schema-evolution#Change Data Feed (CDF) enablement]] |
| **DLZ** | Data Landing Zone (this platform instance) |
| **data-platform** | DLZ code in contracts; ADLS filesystem name |
| **Framework** | Vendor submodule `Framework/` — **do not modify** |
| **Contract** | JSON metadata defining entities, columns, dependencies |
| **CDF Lookup** | Reference data product (`CDFLookup`) — not the whole platform |
| **Egress target** | External system fed *by* the DLZ (Benevity, Milana, PredictX, Cosmos) — never an ingestion source. See [[projects/data-platform-dlz/concepts/egress-pipeline]] |
| **Adatis Framework** | Databricks notebook framework under `0 - Adatis Framework` |
